problem_statement: The GovernedPlanningProfile runtime authorization path needs a schema-backed signed approval intake contract that defines the exact future signed approval values while preserving the absence of a real signed approval witness, signature verification, runtime authorization, and activation.
success_criteria: a deterministic validator emits a signed approval intake contract from the current approval witness template
the checked-in local signed approval intake fixture matches the deterministic produced intake
the schema records AwaitingEvidence and AwaitingSignedApproval for the intake
the intake binds the source approval witness template id and hash
the intake records that decision value, operator identity, source hash acknowledgements, signature reference, and signature verification remain absent
the intake records that signed approval remains absent and runtime authorization gate remains unsatisfied

focused tests cover schema compatibility, fixture parity, source binding, missing required witness values, runtime authority drift, approval collection drift, signature drift, source-template drift, and CLI JSON output
non_goals: do not record a signed operator approval witness
do not accept generic continuation as approval
do not execute, dispatch, replan, replay, or rollback
do not add a router, worker, scheduler, connector, or runtime registration path
do not claim live runtime activation
constraints: the signed approval intake is local, read-only, deterministic, and reversible
the intake contract is not accepted as approval
explicit signed approval values remain required before the runtime authorization gate can be satisfied
runtime activation remains denied until a separate governed activation action is approved
Mfidel atomicity remains preserved because the validator does not normalize or decompose text
evidence_required: schema-backed local signed approval intake fixture
deterministic validator and produced intake parity
SDLC design and security review artifacts
